message:
feat: add semantic boundary policy E2E suite (bd-1now.5.3) with 4-scenario verification and bd-1now.8 evidence

Semantic boundary policy suite (bd-1now.5.3):
- tests/e2e/semantic_boundary_policy_suite.sh: E2E test harness that
  exercises the upgraded check_ownership_violations.py against four
  synthetic workspaces, verifying both allowed and forbidden patterns:
  - allowed_canonical_alignment_cancellation: validates that cancellation
    protocol surfaces in sanctioned paths are accepted
  - allowed_local_model_region_tree: validates local-model region tree
    surfaces pass without violations
  - forbidden_duplicate_family_runtime_cancellation: validates that
    duplicate semantic-family filenames outside sanctioned paths trigger
    OWN-SEMB-002
  - forbidden_internal_boundary_crossing: validates that direct imports
    into internal modules trigger OWN-SEMB-003
- Full artifact bundle: e2e_bundle.json, e2e_log.jsonl, e2e_summary.json,
  e2e_summary.md, stage_results.jsonl, per-scenario stdout.json outputs
- Trace workspaces with synthetic Rust sources and minimal
  capability_ownership_registry.json + contract fixtures for each scenario

bd-1now.8:
- verification_evidence.json for the telemetry bridge verification bead

Issue tracker:
- Update .beads/issues.jsonl with status transitions for semantic boundary
  and telemetry beads

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1now.4.6","title":"Add telemetry end-to-end orchestration scripts and artifact-rich lifecycle logs","description":"Why this exists:
The telemetry refactor changes long-lived runtime behavior that users and operators will experience indirectly through startup/shutdown behavior, missing/persisted events, and diagnosability under failure. A deterministic end-to-end harness is required in addition to unit/integration tests.

What this task must do:
Add end-to-end telemetry orchestration scripts plus artifact-rich logging for the selective Asupersync telemetry cluster.

The E2E harness should exercise representative operator scenarios such as:
- normal engine startup, telemetry ingestion, and orderly shutdown,
- abnormal engine termination / crash while telemetry is active,
- burst traffic that hits the declared backpressure / overflow policy,
- persistence stall or failure injection,
- socket cleanup and worker-resolution behavior after stop/join completes.

Required outputs:
- at least one deterministic script under `tests/e2e/` (or a tightly justified equivalent harness) for the telemetry cluster,
- machine-readable summary output and human-readable detailed logs,
- structured lifecycle / failure events with stable fields that align to the design contract,
- artifact locations future contributors can reuse when diagnosing regressions.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The telemetry cluster has deterministic end-to-end orchestration coverage for normal operation, abnormal termination, backpressure/overflow, persistence failure/stall, and orderly cleanup.
2. The E2E harness emits both machine-readable summaries and human-readable detailed logs/artifacts.
3. The logs contain stable structured fields sufficient for triage, including lifecycle state, reason code, queue state, worker/socket identity, and enough timing context to support later performance characterization.
4. The machine-readable summaries include stable scenario identifiers plus event-accounting receipts, such as expected vs actual accepted/persisted/drained/dropped totals and any policy-authorized loss/shed counts.
5. Artifact locations and schemas are stable and versioned enough that future contributors, the closeout gate, and operator documentation can reuse them directly.
6. Any cargo-heavy build/test work needed for this harness is executed through `rch`.","notes":"The E2E goal is not synthetic coverage for its own sake. It is to prove the operator experience: what happens when the engine exits badly, when the bridge backs up, and what evidence remains afterward.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:32:46.565647748Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-10T09:06:51.388046595Z","closed_at":"2026-03-10T09:06:51.388010688Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","e2e","runtime","self-contained","telemetry","test-obligations","testing","verification-gate"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1now.4.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1now.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:32:46.565647748Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1now.4.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1now.4.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:32:46.976165895Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1now.4.7","title":"Characterize telemetry throughput, backpressure budgets, and operator-facing performance evidence","description":"Why this exists:
The telemetry refactor is justified partly on robustness and correctness, but users also care whether the new ownership model preserves or improves throughput, burst handling, and drain behavior. Without explicit performance evidence, the project could “clean up” the runtime story while silently regressing real operator experience.

What this task must do:
Characterize the refactored telemetry path under realistic load and backpressure conditions, then publish operator-usable performance evidence.

The characterization should cover at minimum:
- steady-state ingestion behavior,
- burst behavior up to and beyond the declared queue/admission budget,
- drain/shutdown time after stop/join,
- queue depth/capacity evolution,
- enqueue/dequeue / persist latency signals,
- any shed/block/batch/fail behavior that the design selected.

Required outputs:
- repeatable load/performance harnesses using the implemented telemetry scripts/logging,
- machine-readable summaries,
- human-readable operator notes explaining what the measured budgets mean in practice,
- stable artifact names that the closeout gate can reference.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The telemetry cluster has repeatable performance/load evidence covering steady-state, burst, overflow/backpressure, and drain/shutdown behavior.
2. The evidence is produced with `rch`-offloaded heavy execution rather than local cargo runs.
3. Machine-readable summaries capture the key user-visible signals: queue depth/capacity, latency or service-time indicators, overflow decisions, drain time, and event-accounting totals needed to interpret throughput without hiding loss; those summaries also carry stable schema/version markers plus scenario identifiers.
4. Human-readable operator notes explain the practical meaning of the measured budgets/thresholds, tradeoffs, and any divergence between ideal throughput and durable persistence guarantees, including any before/after change versus the pre-refactor baseline or clearly declared predecessor behavior; if any budgets are configurable, the notes also explain recommended tuning ranges and consequences, and if they are intentionally fixed, the notes explain why.
5. If the measurements materially contradict the intended design envelope, the bead remains open until the deviation is explained or the implementation/design is corrected.","notes":"This is not a benchmark vanity bead. It exists so the refactor has explicit evidence that it did not trade correctness improvements for unacceptable user-visible throughput or shutdown regressions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:37:46.303723986Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-10T09:31:07.651538735Z","closed_at":"2026-03-10T09:31:07.651510212Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","performance","rch","runtime","self-contained","telemetry","test-obligations","testing","verification-gate"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1now.4.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1now.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:37:46.303723986Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1now.4.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1now.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:37:46.697921814Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1now.4.8","title":"Publish telemetry operator troubleshooting guide and reason-code reference","description":"Why this exists:
Detailed logs and machine-readable summaries only improve user/operator experience if humans can quickly interpret them during incidents or regression triage. The telemetry refactor therefore needs an explicit operator-facing reference aligned to the new states, reason codes, and artifact bundles.

What this task must do:
Publish an operator-facing telemetry troubleshooting/reference guide aligned to the refactored telemetry lifecycle.

The guide should cover at minimum:
- lifecycle states and what they mean,
- overflow/backpressure decision codes and expected operator responses,
- accepted/persisted/drained/dropped accounting fields and how to read them,
- where the E2E, performance, and verification artifacts live,
- common failure modes and the first diagnostic steps to take.

Required outputs:
- a stable docs surface in the repo,
- references that match the actual structured fields and reason codes emitted by the implementation and proof harnesses,
- concise troubleshooting steps that future operators/contributors can follow without reconstructing the architecture from scratch.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The telemetry cluster has an operator-facing reference that maps lifecycle states, reason codes, accounting fields, relevant fixed-vs-configurable budgets/tuning knobs, and artifact locations to concrete interpretation/action.
2. The reference is aligned to the actual machine-readable fields and logs emitted by the proof harnesses rather than aspirational names.
3. The guide covers normal, degraded, and failure scenarios, including overflow/backpressure and abnormal termination.
4. The guide explains how to distinguish policy-authorized shedding, delayed durability, or bounded drain behavior from unexpected loss, duplication, or stuck shutdown using accounting fields, reason codes, and performance-envelope artifacts.
5. The closeout gate can point to this reference as part of the reusable evidence pack for future maintainers/operators.","notes":"This is not generic documentation polish. It is part of the user-facing robustness story: logs are only valuable if operators can decode them quickly under pressure.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:42:37.818221975Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-10T09:32:31.776811476Z","closed_at":"2026-03-10T09:32:31.776783854Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","docs","runtime","self-contained","telemetry","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1now.4.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1now.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:42:37.818221975Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1now.4.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1now.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:42:38.225874797Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1now.4.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1now.4.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:42:38.426406261Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1now.5","title":"Decide local semantic twin policy versus canonical franken_engine/asupersync adapters","description":"Why this exists:
`franken_node` contains local semantic twins of several Asupersync-flavored concepts while `frankenengine-engine` already centralizes canonical upstream primitive imports. If the repo does not define that boundary explicitly, future contributors will oscillate between duplicate-type sprawl and premature migration churn.

What this cluster must do:
- inventory the local semantic twins and classify each one against the canonical upstream ownership boundary,
- explain why each surface should stay local, wrap canonical primitives, migrate only when a live runtime boundary exists, or be forbidden as a duplicate fork,
- add anti-drift proof obligations so the policy remains actionable instead of decaying into prose.

Why this matters:
This is where the project decides what belongs locally for modeling/spec reasons versus what should ultimately collapse onto upstream-owned primitives. The output must be self-contained enough that future maintainers can make consistent decisions without reopening this chat.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The repo has an explicit inventory of local Asupersync-like semantic twins in `franken_node`.
2. Each inventoried surface has a clear policy outcome: keep local, wrap canonical, migrate later under trigger conditions, or forbid as a duplicate primitive fork.
3. The cluster includes anti-drift safeguards, repository-level validation scripts, detailed versioned logging/artifacts, and explicit mapping from policy entries to enforcement/proof surfaces so the policy is enforceable rather than purely narrative.
4. The policy preserves the existing upstream ownership boundary in `frankenengine-engine` and gives future contributors a reliable decision procedure for new concepts, including how to find the rule/check/script that enforces or exemplifies the chosen classification.
5. Any heavy validation needed by this cluster is routed through `rch`.","notes":"This is a semantic-boundary cluster, not a mandate to rewrite everything. A successful outcome may explicitly keep several node-side surfaces local. The important thing is to make those decisions principled, testable where feasible, and reusable by future contributors.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:46:43.827371396Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-10T04:29:50.618078304Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","asupersync","boundaries","plan","runtime","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1now.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1now","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:46:43.827371396Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1now.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1now.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:46:47.157217855Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1now.5.1","title":"Inventory and classify node-side semantic twins against canonical upstream boundaries","description":"Why this exists:
Before the repo can defend a boundary policy, it needs an explicit inventory and classification matrix for the local semantic twins already present.

What this task must do:
Inventory the relevant node-side semantic twins and classify each one against canonical upstream ownership.

The inventory must cover at least:
- `connector/region_ownership.rs`,
- `connector/supervision.rs`,
- `runtime/region_tree.rs`,
- performance budget guards,
- any additional node-side semantic twin discovered while implementing the matrix.

For each surface, capture:
- what problem it solves locally,
- which upstream canonical primitive or adapter it relates to (if any),
- whether it should stay local, wrap canonical ownership, migrate only under a future trigger, or be treated as forbidden duplicate/fork surface,
- what user/operator consequence exists if the surface drifts semantically.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The inventory/classification matrix covers the known node-side semantic twins and any additional relevant surfaces found during implementation.
2. Each entry contains a concrete policy outcome plus the reasoning behind it.
3. Each entry records the user/operator or contributor-facing consequence of semantic drift so the classification is grounded in behavior rather than aesthetics.
4. The matrix includes representative decision examples future contributors can analogize from, including at least one “keep local” case and one “must not fork / should align upstream” case.
5. The matrix is self-contained enough that future contributors can classify a new similar surface by analogy instead of improvising.
6. Each matrix entry points to the intended enforcement/proof surface (checker, test, script, or an explicit justification for why the entry is document-only for now) so the policy is operational rather than merely classificatory.","notes":"Bias toward explicit reasoning over short labels. The goal is for future maintainers to understand why a surface stays local or moves upstream, not just to see a terse classification word.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CalmSnow","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:32:47.431555934Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-10T16:49:22.928556269Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","asupersync","boundaries","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1now.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1now.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:32:47.431555934Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1now.5.2","title":"Add anti-drift safeguards, examples, and consistency checks for semantic-boundary policy","description":"Why this exists:
A classification matrix without anti-drift proof obligations will decay as soon as new local helpers, wrappers, or canonical imports get added under delivery pressure.

What this task must do:
Add the anti-drift layer for the semantic-boundary policy.

Depending on the surfaces involved, that anti-drift layer may include:
- deterministic unit tests or snapshot tests that protect key local-vs-canonical classification tables,
- examples / contributor-facing decision cases that show when a new concept stays local versus when it must reference canonical upstream ownership,
- cheap checkers that fail when a forbidden duplicate/fork pattern or undocumented boundary crossing reappears,
- stable failure logging or reason codes so policy violations are easy to triage.

Why this matters:
This is what makes the semantic-boundary policy reusable and enforceable rather than a one-time document.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The semantic-boundary policy has deterministic anti-drift safeguards appropriate to the actual surfaces involved (tests, snapshots, examples, checkers, or a justified combination).
2. The safeguards make it obvious when a contributor is creating an undocumented duplicate fork or boundary crossing.
3. Failure output is detailed enough for contributors to understand what changed and which policy rule they violated.
4. The safeguards export stable rule ids/messages and, where applicable, machine-readable fields that the repo-level proof harness can reuse instead of inventing a second policy vocabulary.
5. This bead delivers the implementation-layer safeguards; the repo-level scripted/E2E proof lane is tracked separately.
6. If cargo-heavy validation is needed, it is executed through `rch`.","notes":"Do not overfit this to one current file layout. The anti-drift layer should help future contributors make correct ownership decisions even if files move or additional local semantic surfaces appear.

Keep the implementation-layer checks composable so the later repo-level proof harness can reuse the same rule ids, messages, and artifacts instead of inventing parallel policy logic.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:32:47.853428848Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T22:47:20.828695255Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","asupersync","boundaries","self-contained","test-obligations","testing","verification-gate"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1now.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1now.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:32:47.853428848Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1now.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1now.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:32:48.264057922Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1now.5.3","title":"Add repo-level semantic-boundary proof harness with E2E cases and detailed logs","description":"Why this exists:
The semantic-boundary policy will not stay trustworthy if it only exists as a matrix plus low-level checks. The repo needs a scripted proof lane that demonstrates both forbidden and allowed cases with detailed, reusable diagnostics.

What this task must do:
Add a repository-level validation harness for the semantic-boundary policy, with deterministic positive and negative cases.

The harness should exercise at minimum:
- a forbidden duplicate/fork case that must fail,
- an undocumented boundary crossing case that must fail,
- at least one explicitly allowed local-model/spec case that must pass,
- at least one canonical-alignment case that must pass or route to the documented upstream rule.

Required outputs:
- at least one deterministic script under `tests/e2e/` or a tightly justified equivalent repo-level harness,
- machine-readable summary output,
- human-readable detailed logs/artifacts,
- stable reason codes / rule ids that map directly back to the semantic-boundary policy.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The semantic-boundary cluster has deterministic repository-level validation coverage for both forbidden and allowed cases.
2. The harness emits both machine-readable summaries and human-readable detailed logs/artifacts.
3. Failures identify the violated boundary rule with stable reason codes / rule ids and enough context for contributors to fix the issue without policy archaeology.
4. Machine-readable outputs carry stable case identifiers, schema/version markers, and artifact locations that map back to the semantic-boundary matrix and anti-drift rule ids.
5. The harness is reusable by the closeout gate and future regressions, not a one-off proof script.
6. Any cargo-heavy or broad validation required by this lane is executed through `rch`.","notes":"Treat this like a policy E2E lane, not a documentation demo. The point is to prove that the boundary policy can catch real mistakes and preserve intentionally local surfaces without confusing contributors.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:39:56.252355123Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T22:47:21.028884411Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","asupersync","boundaries","e2e","rch","self-contained","test-obligations","testing","verification-gate"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1now.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1now.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:39:56.252355123Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1now.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1now.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:39:56.683187486Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+{"id":"bd-1now.5.3","title":"Add repo-level semantic-boundary proof harness with E2E cases and detailed logs","description":"Why this exists:

 {"id":"bd-1now.6","title":"Deferred trigger: adopt native Asupersync request-region boundaries when a real async server lands","description":"Why this exists:

What this deferred task must preserve for future self:
If `franken_node` later grows a real async network boundary, that is the moment to revisit native Asupersync request-region/capability-narrowing patterns.

Future trigger conditions:
- real socket accept loop or gRPC call boundary is introduced in this crate,
- request cancellation and per-request lifecycle become real runtime concerns,
- middleware begins owning spawn/time/IO authority rather than just metadata,
- the crate needs deterministic request shutdown/drain semantics.

When the trigger fires, the future implementation should evaluate:
- request-region ownership,
- narrowed request `Cx` capability exposure,
- explicit lifecycle/Outcome mapping at transport boundaries,
- whether the crate should own an `AppSpec`-like supervised service topology.

 {"id":"bd-1now.7","title":"Deferred trigger: promote singleton/mutex state owners only when topology justifies actor-style ownership","description":"Why this exists:
The audit found several singleton/mutex-backed or shared-owner surfaces in `franken_node` (for example shared fleet control manager and shared evidence ledger wrappers). Those do not automatically deserve actor/GenServer migration today, but some of them might if the concurrency topology becomes richer.

What this deferred task must preserve for future self:
Record the trigger conditions for promoting singleton/mutex-backed state owners into explicit actor/GenServer/service-lifecycle patterns.

Candidate surfaces to reevaluate later include:
- `api/fleet_quarantine.rs` shared fleet control manager,
- `observability/evidence_ledger.rs` shared ledger wrapper,
- any state owner that later gains reply obligations, named-worker semantics, restart domains, or meaningful concurrent mutation pressure.

Future trigger conditions:
- one natural owner is hidden behind broad shared mutable state,
- callers begin building ad hoc request/reply protocols around channels or locks,
- restart policy or failure isolation becomes important,
- lifecycle/ownership bugs become hard to reason about,
- contention or hidden coordination costs become measurable.

Why this bead exists now:
It gives future contributors a principled promotion rule instead of a style-war rule.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The deferred bead names the main singleton/shared-owner surfaces worth reevaluating later.

+# Tri-Kernel Ownership Contract
+
+## Scope
+
+This contract defines hard ownership boundaries between:
+
+- `franken_engine` (execution kernel)
+- `asupersync` (correctness/control kernel)
+- `franken_node` (product kernel)
+
+Boundary violations are CI-fatal unless covered by a signed, non-expired waiver in
+`docs/governance/ownership_boundary_waivers.json`.
+
+## Ownership Planes
+
+### Execution Plane (`franken_engine`)
+
+Responsibilities:
+
+- Runtime internals and low-level execution behavior
+- Extension host isolation and sandbox substrate
+- Primitive remote/effect execution mechanisms
+
+Prohibited from:
+
+- Embedding product UX/policy semantics
+- Re-defining correctness-level cancellation/epoch protocol rules
+
+### Correctness/Control Plane (`asupersync`)
+
+Responsibilities:
+
+- Cancellation protocol (`request -> drain -> finalize`)
+- Scheduler lane semantics and starvation policy
+- Epoch barriers and transition safety
+- Deterministic evidence/replay correctness contracts
+
+Prohibited from:
+
+- Implementing product UX orchestration directly
+- Bypassing execution kernel public interfaces
+
+### Product Plane (`franken_node`)
+
+Responsibilities:
+
+- Product APIs, operator workflows, diagnostics, and reporting
+- Policy orchestration over correctness/execution APIs
+- Publication and governance surfaces
+
+Prohibited from:
+
+- Re-implementing execution primitives from `franken_engine`
+- Re-implementing correctness protocol internals from `asupersync`
+
+## Boundary Rules
+
+1. Product code may consume correctness behavior only via approved facade APIs.
+2. Product code may consume execution behavior only via approved execution APIs.
+3. Direct imports into internal correctness/execution implementation modules are violations.
+4. Duplicate implementation of ownership-scoped capabilities across kernels is prohibited.
+5. Any exception requires a signed waiver with explicit expiry.
+
+## Canonical Primitive Vocabulary
+
+The canonical primitive list used by workflow-mapping gates is declared in frontmatter under
+`canonical_asupersync_primitives` and currently includes:
+
+- `cx_propagation`
+- `region_ownership_scope`
+- `cancellation_protocol`
+- `obligation_tracking`
+- `remote_computation_registry`
+- `epoch_validity_window`
+- `evidence_ledger_emission`
+
+## Waiver Contract
+
+A waiver record MUST include:
+
+- `waiver_id` (stable identifier)
+- `file` (project-relative path)
+- `boundary` (e.g., `product->correctness-internal`)
+- `rationale`
+- `signed_by` (human approver)
+- `signature` (non-empty approval proof string)
+- `expires_at` (RFC 3339 date-time)
+
+Unsigned or expired waivers fail CI (`OWN-004`).
+
+## Structured Events
+
+- `OWN-001`: boundary check pass
+- `OWN-002`: boundary violation detected
+- `OWN-003`: valid waiver applied
+- `OWN-004`: waiver invalid or expired
+
+## CI Enforcement
+
+Primary gate artifact:
+
+- `artifacts/10.15/ownership_boundary_report.json`
+
+Conformance test surface:
+
+- `tests/conformance/ownership_boundary_checks.rs`
+
+Section evidence outputs:
+
+- `artifacts/section_10_15/bd-1id0/verification_evidence.json`
+- `artifacts/section_10_15/bd-1id0/verification_summary.md`
+
+## Semantic Twin Inventory And Classification Matrix
+
+**Bead:** `bd-1now.5.1`
+
+This section records the current local Asupersync-like semantic twins in
+`franken_node` and classifies each one against the upstream ownership boundary.
+It exists so future contributors do not have to reconstruct the decision from
+chat history or infer it from scattered beads.
+
+When a row points to a heavy cargo validation surface instead of a pure
+doc/script gate, that validation must be run through `rch`.
+
+### Outcome Vocabulary
+
+| Outcome | Meaning | Allowed change shape |
+|---|---|---|
+| `Keep local model` | Local surface remains in `franken_node` because it currently describes product semantics, deterministic modeling, or bounded publication behavior rather than owning a live canonical runtime primitive. | Tighten local invariants, docs, and tests. Do not present it as the canonical primitive. |
+| `Wrap canonical ownership` | Local surface may exist, but its semantics are downstream of an upstream-owned primitive or approved adapter boundary. | Keep the wrapper/facade if useful, but align vocabulary, invariants, and verdicts with the canonical boundary. |
+| `Defer until trigger` | Native Asupersync adoption is premature because the crate does not yet own the required runtime topology. | Keep the local skeleton or documentation only until the named trigger exists. |
+| `Forbid duplicate fork` | A second local implementation would create two competing truths for the same primitive. | Extend an existing sanctioned surface or the upstream adapter instead of adding a new primitive. |
+
+### Decision Procedure For New Surfaces
+
+1. If the surface owns real long-lived concurrency, shutdown, backpressure, restart, or reply obligations today, compare it against the upstream adapter first and prefer `Wrap canonical ownership`.
+2. If the surface is a model/spec/test kernel with no live runtime owner underneath it, prefer `Keep local model`.
+3. If the surface only sketches a future service or actor boundary, classify it as `Defer until trigger` and name the trigger explicitly.
+4. If a new implementation would create a second local source of truth for Cx/region/cancellation/obligation/epoch/lane semantics, classify it as `Forbid duplicate fork`.
+5. Every classification must point to at least one proof surface: spec, checker, unit/integration test, artifact bundle, or an explicit note that the row is document-only for now.

+
+### Representative Analogies For Future Classification
+
+- `connector/region_ownership.rs` is the representative `Keep local model` case: it uses canonical vocabulary, but today it is still a local semantic/model surface rather than the owner of a live async substrate.
+- `connector/cancellation_protocol.rs` is the representative `Wrap canonical ownership` case: product code can expose the contract, but it must not fork the upstream semantics.
+- `api/service.rs` is the representative `Defer until trigger` case: the boundary is conceptually real but not operationally owned yet.
+- "new local primitive file" is the representative `Forbid duplicate fork` case: if the existing sanctioned surfaces are insufficient, the answer is to extend them or the adapter, not create a rival kernel.
+
+### Anti-Drift Rule Catalog
+
+`scripts/check_ownership_violations.py` is the implementation-layer anti-drift
+guard for this policy. It emits the stable rule ids below so later proof
+lanes can reuse the same vocabulary instead of inventing a parallel one.

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""

+
+Reads the canonical capability ownership registry, verifies that the semantic
+boundary contract stays aligned with the implementation-level checker, and
+scans Rust source files for:
+

+- forbidden cross-kernel imports into `*_internal` / `::internal::` modules.
+
+Usage:
+    python3 scripts/check_ownership_violations.py [--json] [--waiver FILE]
+
+Exit codes:
+    0 = PASS (no violations)
+    1 = FAIL (violations detected)
+    2 = ERROR (registry missing, parse error, or policy contract missing)

+
+def _relative_to_root(filepath: Path, project_root: Path = ROOT) -> str:
+    """Return a stable project-relative path when possible."""
+    try:
+        return str(filepath.resolve().relative_to(project_root.resolve()))
+    except ValueError:
+        if filepath.is_absolute():
+            return str(filepath)
+        return str(filepath)
+
+
+def load_registry() -> dict[str, Any]:
+    """Load and validate the capability ownership registry."""
+    if not REGISTRY_PATH.exists():
+        print(f"ERROR: Registry not found: {REGISTRY_PATH}", file=sys.stderr)
+        sys.exit(2)
+    with open(REGISTRY_PATH) as f:
+        return json.load(f)

+def load_policy_contract_text(contract_path: Path = CONTRACT_PATH) -> str:
+    """Load the tri-kernel ownership contract text."""
+    if not contract_path.exists():
+        print(f"ERROR: Policy contract not found: {contract_path}", file=sys.stderr)
+        sys.exit(2)
+    return contract_path.read_text()

+
+def load_waivers(waiver_path: str | None) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
+    """Load waiver file if provided."""
+    if not waiver_path:
+        return []
+    path = Path(waiver_path)
+    if not path.exists():
+        return []
+    with open(path) as f:
+        data = json.load(f)
+    return data.get("waivers", [])

+
+def _make_violation(
+    *,
+    rule_id: str,
+    reason_code: str,
+    file: str,
+    detail: str,
+    remediation: str,
+    severity: str = "error",
+    **extra: Any,
+) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    violation = {
+        "rule_id": rule_id,
+        "reason_code": reason_code,
+        "file": file,
+        "severity": severity,
+        "detail": detail,
+        "remediation": remediation,
+    }
+    violation.update(extra)
+    return violation
+
+
+def check_contract_alignment(contract_text: str) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
+    """Ensure the semantic-boundary contract stays aligned with the checker."""
+    violations = []
+
+    if POLICY_MATRIX_HEADING not in contract_text:
+        violations.append(
+            _make_violation(
+                rule_id="OWN-SEMB-001",
+                reason_code="SEMANTIC_BOUNDARY_CONTRACT_DRIFT",
+                file=CONTRACT_REL_PATH,
+                detail=f"missing contract heading: {POLICY_MATRIX_HEADING}",
+                remediation=(
+                    "Restore the semantic-twin matrix heading in the tri-kernel "
+                    "ownership contract."
+                ),
+            )
+        )
+
+    if RULE_CATALOG_HEADING not in contract_text:
+        violations.append(
+            _make_violation(
+                rule_id="OWN-SEMB-001",
+                reason_code="SEMANTIC_BOUNDARY_CONTRACT_DRIFT",
+                file=CONTRACT_REL_PATH,
+                detail=f"missing contract heading: {RULE_CATALOG_HEADING}",
+                remediation=(
+                    "Restore the anti-drift rule catalog heading in the tri-kernel "
+                    "ownership contract."
+                ),
+            )
+        )
+
+    for rule in RULE_CATALOG:
+        if rule["rule_id"] not in contract_text or rule["reason_code"] not in contract_text:
+            violations.append(
+                _make_violation(
+                    rule_id="OWN-SEMB-001",
+                    reason_code="SEMANTIC_BOUNDARY_CONTRACT_DRIFT",
+                    file=CONTRACT_REL_PATH,
+                    detail=(
+                        "contract missing anti-drift rule catalog entry "
+                        f"{rule['rule_id']} / {rule['reason_code']}"
+                    ),
+                    remediation=(
+                        "Add the missing anti-drift rule catalog entry to the "
+                        "tri-kernel ownership contract."
+                    ),
+                )
+            )
+
+    for family in SEMANTIC_BOUNDARY_FAMILIES:
+        for allowed_path in family["allowed_paths"]:
+            if allowed_path not in contract_text:
+                violations.append(

+                        rule_id="OWN-SEMB-001",
+                        reason_code="SEMANTIC_BOUNDARY_CONTRACT_DRIFT",
+                        file=CONTRACT_REL_PATH,

+
+def check_file_ownership(filepath: Path, registry: dict[str, Any]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
+    """Check a single file for cross-track ownership violations."""
+    violations = []
+    relpath = _relative_to_root(filepath)
+
+    # Determine which track this file belongs to based on path patterns.
+    file_track = None
+    for track, patterns in TRACK_PATH_PATTERNS.items():
+        for pattern in patterns:
+            pattern_re = pattern.replace("*", "[^/]+")
+            if re.match(pattern_re, relpath):
+                file_track = track
+                break
+        if file_track:
+            break
+
+    if not file_track:
+        return []
+
+    # Check if this file implements capabilities owned by another track.
+    for cap in registry.get("capabilities", []):
+        cap_owner = cap["canonical_owner"]
+        owners = cap_owner.split("+")
+
+        if file_track in owners:
+            continue
+        if file_track in cap.get("integration_tracks", []):
+            continue
+
+        domain = cap["domain"].lower()
+        domain_keywords = [
+            word
+            for word in re.split(r"[,/+\s]+", domain)
+            if len(word) > 3 and word not in ("and", "the", "with", "for")
+        ]
+
+        file_stem = filepath.stem.lower()
+        matching_keywords = [kw for kw in domain_keywords if kw in file_stem]
+
+        if matching_keywords:
+            violations.append(
+                {
+                    "rule_id": f"OWNERSHIP-{cap['id']}",
+                    "reason_code": "CROSS_TRACK_CAPABILITY_REDEFINITION",
+                    "file": relpath,
+                    "file_track": file_track,
+                    "capability": cap["id"],
+                    "capability_domain": cap["domain"],
+                    "canonical_owner": cap_owner,
+                    "matching_keywords": matching_keywords,
+                    "severity": "error",
+                    "detail": (
+                        f"{relpath} appears to implement capability {cap['id']} "
+                        f"from canonical track {cap_owner}"
+                    ),
+                    "remediation": (
+                        f"Move implementation to the canonical track {cap_owner} or "
+                        "refactor this file into an integration/adoption role."
+                    ),
+                }
+            )
+
+    return violations
+
+
+def check_semantic_boundary_drift(
+    filepath: Path,
+    project_root: Path = ROOT,
+) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
+    """Check whether a protected semantic family expanded beyond sanctioned paths."""
+    violations = []
+    relpath = _relative_to_root(filepath, project_root)
+    filename = filepath.name
+
+    for family in SEMANTIC_BOUNDARY_FAMILIES:
+        if filename not in family["protected_filenames"]:
+            continue
+        if relpath in family["allowed_paths"]:
+            continue
+        violations.append(
+            _make_violation(
+                rule_id="OWN-SEMB-002",
+                reason_code="UNDOCUMENTED_SEMANTIC_FAMILY",
+                file=relpath,
+                detail=(
+                    f"{filename} belongs to protected semantic family "
+                    f"{family['family_id']} but {relpath} is not in the sanctioned path set"
+                ),
+                remediation=(
+                    "Reuse an existing sanctioned surface or update the semantic-twin "
+                    "matrix and anti-drift checker in the same change."
+                ),
+                family_id=family["family_id"],
+                policy_outcome=family["policy_outcome"],
+                documented_paths=family["allowed_paths"],
+            )
+        )
+
+    return violations
+
+
+def check_forbidden_internal_imports(
+    filepath: Path,
+    project_root: Path = ROOT,
+) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
+    """Reject direct imports into another kernel's internal modules."""
+    text = filepath.read_text(errors="ignore")
+    relpath = _relative_to_root(filepath, project_root)
+    violations = []
+
+    for pattern in FORBIDDEN_INTERNAL_IMPORT_PATTERNS:
+        for match in pattern.finditer(text):
+            snippet = match.group(0).strip()
+            violations.append(
+                _make_violation(
+                    rule_id="OWN-SEMB-003",
+                    reason_code="FORBIDDEN_INTERNAL_BOUNDARY_CROSSING",
+                    file=relpath,
+                    detail=f"forbidden internal import detected: {snippet}",
+                    remediation=(
+                        "Import the public adapter/facade surface instead of reaching "
+                        "into another kernel's internal modules."
+                    ),
+                    import_snippet=snippet,
+                )
+            )
+
+    return violations
+
+
+def _waive(violations: list[dict[str, Any]], waiver_rules: set[tuple[str, str]]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
+    for violation in violations:
+        violation["waived"] = (violation["file"], violation["rule_id"]) in waiver_rules
+    return violations
+
+
+def main() -> None:
+    logger = configure_test_logging("check_ownership_violations")
+    json_output = "--json" in sys.argv
+    waiver_path = None
+    for i, arg in enumerate(sys.argv):
+        if arg == "--waiver" and i + 1 < len(sys.argv):
+            waiver_path = sys.argv[i + 1]
+
+    registry = load_registry()
+    contract_text = load_policy_contract_text()
+    waivers = load_waivers(waiver_path)
+    waiver_rules = {(w["file"], w["rule_id"]) for w in waivers}
+
+    rust_files = sorted(ROOT.rglob("crates/*/src/**/*.rs"))
+
+    contract_violations = _waive(check_contract_alignment(contract_text), waiver_rules)
+    registry_violations: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
+    semantic_violations: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
+    boundary_violations: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
+
+    for rs_file in rust_files:
+        registry_violations.extend(
+            _waive(check_file_ownership(rs_file, registry), waiver_rules)
+        )
+        semantic_violations.extend(
+            _waive(check_semantic_boundary_drift(rs_file), waiver_rules)
+        )
+        boundary_violations.extend(
+            _waive(check_forbidden_internal_imports(rs_file), waiver_rules)
+        )
+
+    all_violations = contract_violations + registry_violations + semantic_violations + boundary_violations
+    active_violations = [v for v in all_violations if not v["waived"]]
+    waived_violations = [v for v in all_violations if v["waived"]]
+
+    verdict = "PASS" if not active_violations else "FAIL"
+
+    report = {
+        "schema_version": "ownership-check/v2",
+        "verdict": verdict,
+        "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
+        "policy_contract": {
+            "path": CONTRACT_REL_PATH,
+            "policy_matrix_heading": POLICY_MATRIX_HEADING,
+            "rule_catalog_heading": RULE_CATALOG_HEADING,
+        },
+        "rule_catalog": RULE_CATALOG,
+        "registry_capabilities": len(registry.get("capabilities", [])),
+        "files_scanned": len(rust_files),
+        "category_counts": {

+            "cross_track_redefinition": len([v for v in registry_violations if not v["waived"]]),
+            "semantic_family_drift": len([v for v in semantic_violations if not v["waived"]]),

+        },
+        "active_violations": len(active_violations),
+        "waived_violations": len(waived_violations),
+        "violations": active_violations,
+        "waived": waived_violations,
+    }

+    if json_output:
+        print(json.dumps(report, indent=2))
+    else:
+        print("=== Semantic Boundary + Ownership CI Gate ===")
+        print(f"Registry capabilities: {report['registry_capabilities']}")
+        print(f"Files scanned: {report['files_scanned']}")
+        print("Active violations by category:")
+        for name, count in report["category_counts"].items():
+            print(f"  - {name}: {count}")
+        print(f"Waived violations: {len(waived_violations)}")
+        print(f"Verdict: {verdict}")
+        if active_violations:
+            print()
+            for violation in active_violations:
+                print(f"  [{violation['rule_id']}] {violation['file']}")
+                print(f"    Reason: {violation['reason_code']}")
+                print(f"    Detail: {violation['detail']}")
+                print(f"    Remediation: {violation['remediation']}")
+
+    if not json_output:
+        logger.info(
+            "ownership gate complete",
+            extra={
+                "verdict": verdict,
+                "active_violations": len(active_violations),
+                "waived_violations": len(waived_violations),
+            },
+        )
+    sys.exit(0 if verdict == "PASS" else 1)
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    main()

+"""Shared test-logging framework for verification scripts.
+
+Provides ``configure_test_logging(script_name)`` which wires up:
+  * Structured JSON or human-readable log lines to **stderr**.
+  * Rotating log files under ``logs/verification/`` (one file per script).
+
+Usage::
+
+    from scripts.lib.test_logger import configure_test_logging
+    logger = configure_test_logging("check_proof_generator")
+    logger.info("starting verification", extra={"check_count": 12})
+
+The returned logger is a stdlib ``logging.Logger`` decorated with a JSON
+formatter when ``--json`` is present in ``sys.argv``.  Human-readable mode
+uses a concise ``%(levelname)s %(name)s: %(message)s`` format.
+
+Log-file rotation keeps the 5 most recent runs (1 MiB each).
+"""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import json
+import logging
+import sys
+from datetime import datetime, timezone
+from logging.handlers import RotatingFileHandler
+from pathlib import Path
+from typing import Any
+
+__all__ = ["configure_test_logging"]
+
+# ── Constants ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+
+_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent
+_LOG_DIR = _ROOT / "logs" / "verification"
+_MAX_BYTES = 1 * 1024 * 1024  # 1 MiB per file
+_BACKUP_COUNT = 5
+
+
+# ── JSON Formatter ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+
+class _JsonFormatter(logging.Formatter):
+    """Emits each log record as a single JSON line."""
+
+    def format(self, record: logging.LogRecord) -> str:
+        payload: dict[str, Any] = {
+            "ts": datetime.fromtimestamp(record.created, tz=timezone.utc).isoformat(),
+            "level": record.levelname,
+            "logger": record.name,
+            "msg": record.getMessage(),
+        }
+        # Merge caller-supplied extras (skip internal fields).
+        for key, value in record.__dict__.items():
+            if key.startswith("_") or key in (
+                "name", "msg", "args", "created", "relativeCreated",
+                "exc_info", "exc_text", "stack_info", "lineno", "funcName",
+                "pathname", "filename", "module", "levelno", "levelname",
+                "msecs", "thread", "threadName", "processName", "process",
+                "message", "taskName",
+            ):
+                continue
+            payload[key] = value
+        return json.dumps(payload, default=str)
+
+
+class _HumanFormatter(logging.Formatter):
+    """Concise single-line format for interactive use."""
+
+    def __init__(self) -> None:
+        super().__init__("%(levelname)-5s %(name)s: %(message)s")
+
+
+# ── Public API ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+
+def configure_test_logging(
+    script_name: str,
+    *,
+    level: int = logging.DEBUG,
+    json_mode: bool | None = None,
+) -> logging.Logger:
+    """Set up and return a logger for *script_name*.
+
+    Parameters
+    ----------
+    script_name:
+        Used as the logger name and the log-file stem
+        (e.g. ``"check_proof_generator"``).
+    level:
+        Minimum logging level.  Defaults to ``DEBUG``.
+    json_mode:
+        Force JSON output.  When ``None`` (the default), JSON mode is
+        auto-detected from ``--json`` in ``sys.argv``.
+
+    Returns
+    -------
+    logging.Logger
+        A fully-configured logger.
+    """
+    if json_mode is None:
+        json_mode = "--json" in sys.argv
+
+    logger = logging.getLogger(script_name)
+    logger.setLevel(level)
+
+    # Avoid duplicate handlers on repeated calls (e.g. in tests).
+    if logger.handlers:
+        return logger
+
+    formatter: logging.Formatter = (
+        _JsonFormatter() if json_mode else _HumanFormatter()
+    )
+
+    # 1. stderr handler
+    stderr_handler = logging.StreamHandler(sys.stderr)
+    stderr_handler.setLevel(level)
+    stderr_handler.setFormatter(formatter)
+    logger.addHandler(stderr_handler)
+
+    # 2. Rotating file handler (best-effort — skip if dir is unwritable).
+    try:
+        _LOG_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+        log_path = _LOG_DIR / f"{script_name}.log"
+        file_handler = RotatingFileHandler(
+            log_path,
+            maxBytes=_MAX_BYTES,
+            backupCount=_BACKUP_COUNT,
+            encoding="utf-8",
+        )
+        file_handler.setLevel(level)
+        file_handler.setFormatter(_JsonFormatter())  # always JSON on disk
+        logger.addHandler(file_handler)
+    except OSError:
+        # Non-fatal: logging to file is optional.
+        pass
+
+    # Prevent propagation to root logger (avoids duplicate output).
+    logger.propagate = False
+
+    return logger
+
+
+# ── Self-test ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+
+def self_test() -> None:
+    """Minimal smoke-test invoked by ``python -m scripts.lib.test_logger``."""
+    log = configure_test_logging("test_logger_selftest", json_mode=False)
+    log.info("human-mode line")
+    log.debug("debug detail", extra={"key": "value"})
+
+    log2 = configure_test_logging("test_logger_selftest_json", json_mode=True)
+    log2.info("json-mode line", extra={"count": 42})
+
+    # Verify idempotency — second call must not add duplicate handlers.
+    log3 = configure_test_logging("test_logger_selftest")
+    assert len(log3.handlers) == len(log.handlers), "handler count changed on re-init"
+
+    print("self_test PASSED")
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    self_test()

+# Tri-Kernel Ownership Contract
+
+## Scope
+
+This contract defines hard ownership boundaries between:
+
+- `franken_engine` (execution kernel)
+- `asupersync` (correctness/control kernel)
+- `franken_node` (product kernel)
+
+Boundary violations are CI-fatal unless covered by a signed, non-expired waiver in
+`docs/governance/ownership_boundary_waivers.json`.
+
+## Ownership Planes
+
+### Execution Plane (`franken_engine`)
+
+Responsibilities:
+
+- Runtime internals and low-level execution behavior
+- Extension host isolation and sandbox substrate
+- Primitive remote/effect execution mechanisms
+
+Prohibited from:
+
+- Embedding product UX/policy semantics
+- Re-defining correctness-level cancellation/epoch protocol rules
+
+### Correctness/Control Plane (`asupersync`)
+
+Responsibilities:
+
+- Cancellation protocol (`request -> drain -> finalize`)
+- Scheduler lane semantics and starvation policy
+- Epoch barriers and transition safety
+- Deterministic evidence/replay correctness contracts
+
+Prohibited from:
+
+- Implementing product UX orchestration directly
+- Bypassing execution kernel public interfaces
+
+### Product Plane (`franken_node`)
+
+Responsibilities:
+
+- Product APIs, operator workflows, diagnostics, and reporting
+- Policy orchestration over correctness/execution APIs
+- Publication and governance surfaces
+
+Prohibited from:
+
+- Re-implementing execution primitives from `franken_engine`
+- Re-implementing correctness protocol internals from `asupersync`
+
+## Boundary Rules
+
+1. Product code may consume correctness behavior only via approved facade APIs.
+2. Product code may consume execution behavior only via approved execution APIs.
+3. Direct imports into internal correctness/execution implementation modules are violations.
+4. Duplicate implementation of ownership-scoped capabilities across kernels is prohibited.
+5. Any exception requires a signed waiver with explicit expiry.
+
+## Canonical Primitive Vocabulary
+
+The canonical primitive list used by workflow-mapping gates is declared in frontmatter under
+`canonical_asupersync_primitives` and currently includes:
+
+- `cx_propagation`
+- `region_ownership_scope`
+- `cancellation_protocol`
+- `obligation_tracking`
+- `remote_computation_registry`
+- `epoch_validity_window`
+- `evidence_ledger_emission`
+
+## Waiver Contract
+
+A waiver record MUST include:
+
+- `waiver_id` (stable identifier)
+- `file` (project-relative path)
+- `boundary` (e.g., `product->correctness-internal`)
+- `rationale`
+- `signed_by` (human approver)
+- `signature` (non-empty approval proof string)
+- `expires_at` (RFC 3339 date-time)
+
+Unsigned or expired waivers fail CI (`OWN-004`).
+
+## Structured Events
+
+- `OWN-001`: boundary check pass
+- `OWN-002`: boundary violation detected
+- `OWN-003`: valid waiver applied
+- `OWN-004`: waiver invalid or expired
+
+## CI Enforcement
+
+Primary gate artifact:
+
+- `artifacts/10.15/ownership_boundary_report.json`
+
+Conformance test surface:
+
+- `tests/conformance/ownership_boundary_checks.rs`
+
+Section evidence outputs:
+
+- `artifacts/section_10_15/bd-1id0/verification_evidence.json`

+## Semantic Twin Inventory And Classification Matrix
+
+**Bead:** `bd-1now.5.1`
+
+This section records the current local Asupersync-like semantic twins in
+`franken_node` and classifies each one against the upstream ownership boundary.
+It exists so future contributors do not have to reconstruct the decision from
+chat history or infer it from scattered beads.
+
+When a row points to a heavy cargo validation surface instead of a pure
+doc/script gate, that validation must be run through `rch`.
+
+### Outcome Vocabulary
+
+| Outcome | Meaning | Allowed change shape |
+|---|---|---|
+| `Keep local model` | Local surface remains in `franken_node` because it currently describes product semantics, deterministic modeling, or bounded publication behavior rather than owning a live canonical runtime primitive. | Tighten local invariants, docs, and tests. Do not present it as the canonical primitive. |
+| `Wrap canonical ownership` | Local surface may exist, but its semantics are downstream of an upstream-owned primitive or approved adapter boundary. | Keep the wrapper/facade if useful, but align vocabulary, invariants, and verdicts with the canonical boundary. |

+### Representative Analogies For Future Classification
+
+- `connector/region_ownership.rs` is the representative `Keep local model` case: it uses canonical vocabulary, but today it is still a local semantic/model surface rather than the owner of a live async substrate.
+- `connector/cancellation_protocol.rs` is the representative `Wrap canonical ownership` case: product code can expose the contract, but it must not fork the upstream semantics.
+- `api/service.rs` is the representative `Defer until trigger` case: the boundary is conceptually real but not operationally owned yet.
+- "new local primitive file" is the representative `Forbid duplicate fork` case: if the existing sanctioned surfaces are insufficient, the answer is to extend them or the adapter, not create a rival kernel.

+### Anti-Drift Rule Catalog
+
+`scripts/check_ownership_violations.py` is the implementation-layer anti-drift
+guard for this policy. It emits the stable rule ids below so later proof
+lanes can reuse the same vocabulary instead of inventing a parallel one.
+
+| Rule ID | Reason code | Failure condition | Required contributor action |
+|---|---|---|---|
+| `OWN-SEMB-001` | `SEMANTIC_BOUNDARY_CONTRACT_DRIFT` | The ownership contract is missing the semantic-twin matrix, the anti-drift rule catalog, or a sanctioned protected path that the checker expects. | Update this contract and the checker in the same change so the documented and machine-readable policy stay aligned. |
+| `OWN-SEMB-002` | `UNDOCUMENTED_SEMANTIC_FAMILY` | A protected semantic-family filename appears outside the sanctioned path set documented in this contract. | Reuse or extend an existing sanctioned surface, or update the matrix/rule catalog explicitly before landing a new family member. |
+| `OWN-SEMB-003` | `FORBIDDEN_INTERNAL_BOUNDARY_CROSSING` | `franken_node` imports another kernel's `*_internal` or `::internal::` modules directly. | Route through the approved public adapter/facade surface instead of reaching into internals. |

+    if not file_track:

+    for cap in registry.get("capabilities", []):
+        cap_owner = cap["canonical_owner"]
+        owners = cap_owner.split("+")
+
+        if file_track in owners:
+            continue
+        if file_track in cap.get("integration_tracks", []):
+            continue
+
+        domain = cap["domain"].lower()
+        domain_keywords = [
+            word
+            for word in re.split(r"[,/+\s]+", domain)
+            if len(word) > 3 and word not in ("and", "the", "with", "for")
+        ]
+
+        file_stem = filepath.stem.lower()
+        matching_keywords = [kw for kw in domain_keywords if kw in file_stem]
+
+        if matching_keywords:
+            violations.append(
+                {

+                    "reason_code": "CROSS_TRACK_CAPABILITY_REDEFINITION",
+                    "file": relpath,
+                    "file_track": file_track,

+                    file=relpath,

+                    remediation=(
+                        "Import the public adapter/facade surface instead of reaching "
+                        "into another kernel's internal modules."
+                    ),
+                    import_snippet=snippet,
+                )
+            )
+
+    return violations
+
+
+def _waive(violations: list[dict[str, Any]], waiver_rules: set[tuple[str, str]]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
+    for violation in violations:
+        violation["waived"] = (violation["file"], violation["rule_id"]) in waiver_rules
+    return violations
+
+
+def main() -> None:
+    logger = configure_test_logging("check_ownership_violations")
+    json_output = "--json" in sys.argv
+    waiver_path = None
+    for i, arg in enumerate(sys.argv):
+        if arg == "--waiver" and i + 1 < len(sys.argv):
+            waiver_path = sys.argv[i + 1]
+
+    registry = load_registry()
+    contract_text = load_policy_contract_text()
+    waivers = load_waivers(waiver_path)
+    waiver_rules = {(w["file"], w["rule_id"]) for w in waivers}
+
+    rust_files = sorted(ROOT.rglob("crates/*/src/**/*.rs"))
+
+    contract_violations = _waive(check_contract_alignment(contract_text), waiver_rules)
+    registry_violations: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
+    semantic_violations: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
+    boundary_violations: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
+
+    for rs_file in rust_files:
+        registry_violations.extend(
+            _waive(check_file_ownership(rs_file, registry), waiver_rules)
+        )
+        semantic_violations.extend(
+            _waive(check_semantic_boundary_drift(rs_file), waiver_rules)
+        )
+        boundary_violations.extend(
+            _waive(check_forbidden_internal_imports(rs_file), waiver_rules)
+        )
+
+    all_violations = contract_violations + registry_violations + semantic_violations + boundary_violations
+    active_violations = [v for v in all_violations if not v["waived"]]
+    waived_violations = [v for v in all_violations if v["waived"]]
+
+    verdict = "PASS" if not active_violations else "FAIL"
+
+    report = {
+        "schema_version": "ownership-check/v2",
+        "verdict": verdict,
+        "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
+        "policy_contract": {
+            "path": CONTRACT_REL_PATH,
+            "policy_matrix_heading": POLICY_MATRIX_HEADING,
+            "rule_catalog_heading": RULE_CATALOG_HEADING,
+        },
+        "rule_catalog": RULE_CATALOG,
+        "registry_capabilities": len(registry.get("capabilities", [])),
+        "files_scanned": len(rust_files),
+        "category_counts": {
+            "contract_alignment": len([v for v in contract_violations if not v["waived"]]),
+            "cross_track_redefinition": len([v for v in registry_violations if not v["waived"]]),
+            "semantic_family_drift": len([v for v in semantic_violations if not v["waived"]]),
+            "internal_boundary_crossing": len([v for v in boundary_violations if not v["waived"]]),
+        },
+        "active_violations": len(active_violations),
+        "waived_violations": len(waived_violations),
+        "violations": active_violations,
+        "waived": waived_violations,
+    }
+
+    if json_output:
+        print(json.dumps(report, indent=2))
+    else:
+        print("=== Semantic Boundary + Ownership CI Gate ===")
+        print(f"Registry capabilities: {report['registry_capabilities']}")
+        print(f"Files scanned: {report['files_scanned']}")
+        print("Active violations by category:")
+        for name, count in report["category_counts"].items():
+            print(f"  - {name}: {count}")
+        print(f"Waived violations: {len(waived_violations)}")
+        print(f"Verdict: {verdict}")
+        if active_violations:
+            print()
+            for violation in active_violations:
+                print(f"  [{violation['rule_id']}] {violation['file']}")
+                print(f"    Reason: {violation['reason_code']}")
+                print(f"    Detail: {violation['detail']}")
+                print(f"    Remediation: {violation['remediation']}")
+
+    if not json_output:
+        logger.info(
+            "ownership gate complete",
+            extra={
+                "verdict": verdict,
+                "active_violations": len(active_violations),
+                "waived_violations": len(waived_violations),
+            },
+        )
+    sys.exit(0 if verdict == "PASS" else 1)
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    main()

+"""Shared test-logging framework for verification scripts.
+
+Provides ``configure_test_logging(script_name)`` which wires up:
+  * Structured JSON or human-readable log lines to **stderr**.
+  * Rotating log files under ``logs/verification/`` (one file per script).
+
+Usage::
+
+    from scripts.lib.test_logger import configure_test_logging
+    logger = configure_test_logging("check_proof_generator")
+    logger.info("starting verification", extra={"check_count": 12})
+
+The returned logger is a stdlib ``logging.Logger`` decorated with a JSON
+formatter when ``--json`` is present in ``sys.argv``.  Human-readable mode
+uses a concise ``%(levelname)s %(name)s: %(message)s`` format.
+
+Log-file rotation keeps the 5 most recent runs (1 MiB each).
+"""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import json
+import logging
+import sys
+from datetime import datetime, timezone
+from logging.handlers import RotatingFileHandler
+from pathlib import Path
+from typing import Any
+
+__all__ = ["configure_test_logging"]
+
+# ── Constants ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+
+_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent
+_LOG_DIR = _ROOT / "logs" / "verification"
+_MAX_BYTES = 1 * 1024 * 1024  # 1 MiB per file
+_BACKUP_COUNT = 5
+
+
+# ── JSON Formatter ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+
+class _JsonFormatter(logging.Formatter):
+    """Emits each log record as a single JSON line."""
+
+    def format(self, record: logging.LogRecord) -> str:
+        payload: dict[str, Any] = {
+            "ts": datetime.fromtimestamp(record.created, tz=timezone.utc).isoformat(),
+            "level": record.levelname,
+            "logger": record.name,
+            "msg": record.getMessage(),
+        }
+        # Merge caller-supplied extras (skip internal fields).
+        for key, value in record.__dict__.items():
+            if key.startswith("_") or key in (
+                "name", "msg", "args", "created", "relativeCreated",
+                "exc_info", "exc_text", "stack_info", "lineno", "funcName",
+                "pathname", "filename", "module", "levelno", "levelname",
+                "msecs", "thread", "threadName", "processName", "process",
+                "message", "taskName",
+            ):
+                continue
+            payload[key] = value
+        return json.dumps(payload, default=str)
+
+
+class _HumanFormatter(logging.Formatter):
+    """Concise single-line format for interactive use."""
+
+    def __init__(self) -> None:
+        super().__init__("%(levelname)-5s %(name)s: %(message)s")
+
+
+# ── Public API ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+
+def configure_test_logging(
+    script_name: str,
+    *,
+    level: int = logging.DEBUG,
+    json_mode: bool | None = None,
+) -> logging.Logger:
+    """Set up and return a logger for *script_name*.
+
+    Parameters
+    ----------
+    script_name:
+        Used as the logger name and the log-file stem
+        (e.g. ``"check_proof_generator"``).
+    level:
+        Minimum logging level.  Defaults to ``DEBUG``.
+    json_mode:
+        Force JSON output.  When ``None`` (the default), JSON mode is
+        auto-detected from ``--json`` in ``sys.argv``.
+
+    Returns
+    -------
+    logging.Logger
+        A fully-configured logger.
+    """
+    if json_mode is None:
+        json_mode = "--json" in sys.argv
+
+    logger = logging.getLogger(script_name)
+    logger.setLevel(level)
+
+    # Avoid duplicate handlers on repeated calls (e.g. in tests).
+    if logger.handlers:
+        return logger
+
+    formatter: logging.Formatter = (
+        _JsonFormatter() if json_mode else _HumanFormatter()
+    )
+
+    # 1. stderr handler
+    stderr_handler = logging.StreamHandler(sys.stderr)
+    stderr_handler.setLevel(level)
+    stderr_handler.setFormatter(formatter)
+    logger.addHandler(stderr_handler)
+
+    # 2. Rotating file handler (best-effort — skip if dir is unwritable).
+    try:
+        _LOG_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+        log_path = _LOG_DIR / f"{script_name}.log"
+        file_handler = RotatingFileHandler(
+            log_path,
+            maxBytes=_MAX_BYTES,
+            backupCount=_BACKUP_COUNT,
+            encoding="utf-8",
+        )
+        file_handler.setLevel(level)
+        file_handler.setFormatter(_JsonFormatter())  # always JSON on disk
+        logger.addHandler(file_handler)
+    except OSError:
+        # Non-fatal: logging to file is optional.
+        pass
+
+    # Prevent propagation to root logger (avoids duplicate output).
+    logger.propagate = False
+
+    return logger
+
+
+# ── Self-test ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+
+def self_test() -> None:
+    """Minimal smoke-test invoked by ``python -m scripts.lib.test_logger``."""
+    log = configure_test_logging("test_logger_selftest", json_mode=False)
+    log.info("human-mode line")
+    log.debug("debug detail", extra={"key": "value"})
+
+    log2 = configure_test_logging("test_logger_selftest_json", json_mode=True)
+    log2.info("json-mode line", extra={"count": 42})
+
+    # Verify idempotency — second call must not add duplicate handlers.
+    log3 = configure_test_logging("test_logger_selftest")
+    assert len(log3.handlers) == len(log.handlers), "handler count changed on re-init"
+
+    print("self_test PASSED")
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    self_test()

+# Tri-Kernel Ownership Contract
+
+## Scope
+
+This contract defines hard ownership boundaries between:
+
+- `franken_engine` (execution kernel)
+- `asupersync` (correctness/control kernel)
+- `franken_node` (product kernel)
+
+Boundary violations are CI-fatal unless covered by a signed, non-expired waiver in
+`docs/governance/ownership_boundary_waivers.json`.
+
+## Ownership Planes
+
+### Execution Plane (`franken_engine`)
+
+Responsibilities:
+
+- Runtime internals and low-level execution behavior
+- Extension host isolation and sandbox substrate
+- Primitive remote/effect execution mechanisms
+
+Prohibited from:
+
+- Embedding product UX/policy semantics
+- Re-defining correctness-level cancellation/epoch protocol rules
+
+### Correctness/Control Plane (`asupersync`)
+
+Responsibilities:
+
+- Cancellation protocol (`request -> drain -> finalize`)
+- Scheduler lane semantics and starvation policy
+- Epoch barriers and transition safety
+- Deterministic evidence/replay correctness contracts
+
+Prohibited from:
+
+- Implementing product UX orchestration directly
+- Bypassing execution kernel public interfaces
+
+### Product Plane (`franken_node`)
+
+Responsibilities:
+
+- Product APIs, operator workflows, diagnostics, and reporting
+- Policy orchestration over correctness/execution APIs
+- Publication and governance surfaces
+
+Prohibited from:
+
+- Re-implementing execution primitives from `franken_engine`
+- Re-implementing correctness protocol internals from `asupersync`
+
+## Boundary Rules
+
+1. Product code may consume correctness behavior only via approved facade APIs.
+2. Product code may consume execution behavior only via approved execution APIs.
+3. Direct imports into internal correctness/execution implementation modules are violations.
+4. Duplicate implementation of ownership-scoped capabilities across kernels is prohibited.
+5. Any exception requires a signed waiver with explicit expiry.
+
+## Canonical Primitive Vocabulary
+
+The canonical primitive list used by workflow-mapping gates is declared in frontmatter under
+`canonical_asupersync_primitives` and currently includes:
+
+- `cx_propagation`
+- `region_ownership_scope`
+- `cancellation_protocol`
+- `obligation_tracking`
+- `remote_computation_registry`
+- `epoch_validity_window`
+- `evidence_ledger_emission`
+
+## Waiver Contract
+
+A waiver record MUST include:
+
+- `waiver_id` (stable identifier)
+- `file` (project-relative path)
+- `boundary` (e.g., `product->correctness-internal`)
+- `rationale`
+- `signed_by` (human approver)
+- `signature` (non-empty approval proof string)
+- `expires_at` (RFC 3339 date-time)
+
+Unsigned or expired waivers fail CI (`OWN-004`).
+
+## Structured Events
+
+- `OWN-001`: boundary check pass
+- `OWN-002`: boundary violation detected
+- `OWN-003`: valid waiver applied
+- `OWN-004`: waiver invalid or expired
+
+## CI Enforcement
+
+Primary gate artifact:
+
+- `artifacts/10.15/ownership_boundary_report.json`
+
+Conformance test surface:
+
+- `tests/conformance/ownership_boundary_checks.rs`
+
+Section evidence outputs:
+
+- `artifacts/section_10_15/bd-1id0/verification_evidence.json`
+- `artifacts/section_10_15/bd-1id0/verification_summary.md`

+`franken_node` and classifies each one against the upstream ownership boundary.
+It exists so future contributors do not have to reconstruct the decision from
+chat history or infer it from scattered beads.

+When a row points to a heavy cargo validation surface instead of a pure

+| Outcome | Meaning | Allowed change shape |

+| `Keep local model` | Local surface remains in `franken_node` because it currently describes product semantics, deterministic modeling, or bounded publication behavior rather than owning a live canonical runtime primitive. | Tighten local invariants, docs, and tests. +| Any new local file that re-defines Cx/region/cancellation/obligation/epoch/lane semantics outside the sanctioned surfaces above | All canonical primitives listed in this contract frontmatter | None; this would be a second truth, not a needed product surface. | `Forbid duplicate fork` | No trigger. Extend an existing sanctioned wrapper/model or the upstream adapter instead. | Two competing semantic kernels make verification, operator docs, and future migrations incoherent. | This contract, `docs/architecture/blueprint.md`, and the follow-on anti-drift lane `bd-1now.5.2` |
+
+### Representative Analogies For Future Classification
+
+- `connector/region_ownership.rs` is the representative `Keep local model` case: it uses canonical vocabulary, but today it is still a local semantic/model surface rather than the owner of a live async substrate.
+- `connector/cancellation_protocol.rs` is the representative `Wrap canonical ownership` case: product code can expose the contract, but it must not fork the upstream semantics.
+- `api/service.rs` is the representative `Defer until trigger` case: the boundary is conceptually real but not operationally owned yet.
+- "new local primitive file" is the representative `Forbid duplicate fork` case: if the existing sanctioned surfaces are insufficient, the answer is to extend them or the adapter, not create a rival kernel.
+
+### Anti-Drift Rule Catalog
+
+`scripts/check_ownership_violations.py` is the implementation-layer anti-drift
+guard for this policy. It emits the stable rule ids below so later proof
+lanes can reuse the same vocabulary instead of inventing a parallel one.
+
+| Rule ID | Reason code | Failure condition | Required contributor action |
+|---|---|---|---|
+| `OWN-SEMB-001` | `SEMANTIC_BOUNDARY_CONTRACT_DRIFT` | The ownership contract is missing the semantic-twin matrix, the anti-drift rule catalog, or a sanctioned protected path that the checker expects. | Update this contract and the checker in the same change so the documented and machine-readable policy stay aligned. |
+| `OWN-SEMB-002` | `UNDOCUMENTED_SEMANTIC_FAMILY` | A protected semantic-family filename appears outside the sanctioned path set documented in this contract. | Reuse or extend an existing sanctioned surface, or update the matrix/rule catalog explicitly before landing a new family member. |
+| `OWN-SEMB-003` | `FORBIDDEN_INTERNAL_BOUNDARY_CROSSING` | `franken_node` imports another kernel's `*_internal` or `::internal::` modules directly. | Route through the approved public adapter/facade surface instead of reaching into internals. |

+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""
+Duplicate-implementation and semantic-boundary anti-drift CI gate for franken_node.
+
+Reads the canonical capability ownership registry, verifies that the semantic
+boundary contract stays aligned with the implementation-level checker, and
+scans Rust source files for:

+- prohibited duplicate implementations of canonically owned capabilities,

+- forbidden cross-kernel imports into `*_internal` / `::internal::` modules.
+
+Usage:
+    python3 scripts/check_ownership_violations.py [--json] [--waiver FILE]
+
+Exit codes:
+    0 = PASS (no violations)
+    1 = FAIL (violations detected)
+    2 = ERROR (registry missing, parse error, or policy contract missing)

+# Semantic keyword patterns that indicate implementation (not just integration/reference)
+IMPLEMENTATION_INDICATORS = [
